sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports sustaining a shoulder injury while playing tackle football. He describes pain in the right acromioclavicular joint. He has taken Tylenol for pain relief. He denies fever or chills. He reports a slight headache. He reports no numbness or tingling in his fingers. He states his symptoms are currently the same as when they initially occurred.
medical_concepts: pain in the right acromioclavicular joint

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's vital signs are stable: blood pressure is 122/76 mmHg, heart rate is 82 bpm, and oxygen saturation is 95% on room air. The cervical spine exam reveals no tenderness. The right shoulder x-ray shows an acute acromioclavicular joint fracture. The right shoulder x-ray also reveals a palpable radial pulse.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has an acute acromioclavicular joint fracture of the right shoulder. The injury is likely related to the recent tackle football activity.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe meloxicam 15 mg once daily. Order a CT scan of the right shoulder to further evaluate the joint. Refer to physical therapy for shoulder strengthening and rehabilitation. Provide a sling for support. Advise the patient to avoid playing tackle football for a period of time while the fracture heals.

medical_concepts: pain in the right acromioclavicular joint
